(born 20 October 1987) is a [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player, born in Karachi, [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan").[[1]](#cite_note-pb-1) Bashir was crowned the national champion in 2009, winning the women's singles and doubles titles.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Palwasha has been representing Pakistan for several years at different international competitions. Initially, she was a rising star on the national level, winning back-to-back national titles. She won the bronze medal at the [2010 South Asian Games](/wiki/2010_South_Asian_Games "2010 South Asian Games") held in [Dhaka](/wiki/Dhaka "Dhaka").[[1]](#cite_note-pb-1) This achievement led her to a greater fame and acknowledgement in Pakistan. Bashir has represented her country at the [2014 Asian Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2014_Asian_Games "Badminton at the 2014 Asian Games"),[[3]](#cite_note-3) as well as the [2014](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2014_Commonwealth_Games "Badminton at the 2014 Commonwealth Games") and [2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "Badminton at the 2018 Commonwealth Games").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) She has been coached by Raziuddin Ahmad.

["Wajid, Palwasha win National Badminton titles"](https://nation.com.pk/23-Feb-2009/wajid-palwasha-win-national-badminton-titles). [The Nation](/wiki/The_Nation_(Pakistan) "The Nation (Pakistan)"). Retrieved 12 April 2018.

["2014 Incheon Asian Games: And so it ends, in disappointment"](https://tribune.com.pk/story/771505/2014-incheon-asian-games-and-so-it-ends-in-disappointment/). [The Express Tribune](/wiki/The_Express_Tribune "The Express Tribune"). Retrieved 12 April 2018.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Pakistani badminton player

Palwasha Bashir

**Palwasha Bashir** (born 20 October 1987) is a [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player, born in Karachi, [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan").[[1]](#cite_note-pb-1) Bashir was crowned the national champion in 2009, winning the women's singles and doubles titles.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Palwasha has been representing Pakistan for several years at different international competitions. Initially, she was a rising star on the national level, winning back-to-back national titles. She won the bronze medal at the [2010 South Asian Games](/wiki/2010_South_Asian_Games "2010 South Asian Games") held in [Dhaka](/wiki/Dhaka "Dhaka").[[1]](#cite_note-pb-1) This achievement led her to a greater fame and acknowledgement in Pakistan. Bashir has represented her country at the [2014 Asian Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2014_Asian_Games "Badminton at the 2014 Asian Games"),[[3]](#cite_note-3) as well as the [2014](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2014_Commonwealth_Games "Badminton at the 2014 Commonwealth Games") and [2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "Badminton at the 2018 Commonwealth Games").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) She has been coached by Raziuddin Ahmad.
